root_cause.headline: Tier-2 commercial wind policies in coastal Texas and Louisiana share an outdated debris-removal sublimit that has not been re-tuned since 2024
root_cause.detail: 22 of 34 over-model Hurricane-Beryl claims share a single policy clause signature: a debris-removal sublimit set at $25K per occurrence, unchanged since the 2024 policy year. Post-Beryl, actual debris-removal costs ran $38–52K in coastal counties, absorbing more incurred loss than the cat model assumed. Tier-1 and tier-3 policies in the same counties show normal severity ratios because their sublimits were updated at the 2025 renewal cycle. The fix is a clause-level pricing adjustment at the next renewal — not a model failure.
root_cause.affected_cohort: Tier-2 commercial wind, TX and LA coastal counties, debris_removal_sublimit = $25K

without_wizard.label: Without dbt-wizard
without_wizard.summary: Question enters the actuarial backlog. Reserves Committee meets without the root-cause finding.
narrative: VP of Claims files the request. It enters the actuarial analytics backlog at priority B.
Data engineer scopes the work Monday morning — four days after the committee.
Engineer writes SQL from scratch, joining four gold tables by hand.
Tests, YAML, and contract get bolted on at PR time — sometimes.
Actuarial team cannot isolate the debris-removal sublimit clause before the committee.
Reserve adjustment is delayed. Quarter-end close carries a $8.4M open item.

with_wizard.label: With dbt-wizard
with_wizard.summary: Four sub-agents surface the upstreams, author the SQL, write the tests, and materialize the asset in 90 seconds.
narrative: Explorer runs status and search. Returns the four gold upstream candidates.
Summary runs describe and lineage. Confirms join path and names the new grain.
Worker runs dbt_show on the Beryl coastal slice. Validates the proposed grain.
Worker authors fct_cat_wind_severity_by_tier_region_daily.sql with CTEs and final SELECT.
Verification writes the YAML — description, schema contract, 9 tests, ownership.
Worker materializes the table to Snowflake. Verification confirms downstream lineage.
Reserves Committee receives the root-cause finding and approves the $8.4M adjustment before quarter-end.

hero.label: Root cause identified before the Reserves Committee meets — $8.4M reserve adjustment made before quarter-end
hero.note: From VP of Claims question to materialized Snowflake gold table — versioned, tested, documented, lineage-tracked. The actuarial team now knows the debris-removal sublimit clause is the driver. The fix is a pricing adjustment at the next renewal, not a catastrophe model failure.
